sentences.sentence: . As in New Delhi and Phoenix, policymakers worldwide wield great power over how water resources and managed.
sentences.translation: 与新德里和菲尼克斯的情况一样，世界各地的政策制定者对水资源如何管理拥有巨大的权力。

structure.text: As in New Delhi and Phoenix
over how water resources and managed

difficult_points: As in New Delhi and Phoenix 表示这里的情况与这两个城市相似。
how 引导的内容说明政策制定者的权力涉及水资源的管理方式；原文的 and managed 存在转录残缺，需按原文保留。
wield great power over 表示“对……掌握巨大权力”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 世界各地的政策制定者

sentences.sentence: Wise use of such power will become increasingly important as the years go by because the world's demand for freshwater is currently overtaking its ready supply in many places, and this situation shows no sign of abating.
sentences.translation: 随着时间推移，明智运用这种权力将变得日益重要，因为目前世界许多地方的淡水需求正超过现有供应，而且这种局面没有缓解的迹象。

structure.text: because the world's demand for freshwater is currently overtaking its ready supply in many places
and this situation shows no sign of abating

difficult_points: as the years go by 是时间状语从句，表示“随着时间推移”。
because 引导原因状语从句，说明明智用权为何越来越重要。
this situation 回指淡水需求超过现有供应的局面；of abating 修饰 sign。

sentences.sentence: . That the problem is well-known makes it no less disturbing: today one out of six people, more than a billion, suffer inadequate access to safe freshwater.
sentences.translation: 这个问题广为人知，却丝毫没有减轻其令人不安的程度：如今每六人中就有一人，也就是十亿多人，无法充分获得安全淡水。

structure.text: today one out of six people
suffer inadequate access to safe freshwater

difficult_points: That the problem is well-known 是 that 引导的主语从句。
no less disturbing 表示“并不因此少一分令人不安”，并非程度较轻。
more than a billion 是 one out of six people 的同位补充说明。

sentences.sentence: By 2025, according to data released by the United Nations, the freshwater resources of more than half the countries across the globe will undergo either stress- for example, when people increasingly demand more water than is available or safe for use-or outright shortages.
sentences.translation: 根据联合国发布的数据，到2025年，全球一半以上国家的淡水资源将面临压力——例如人们日益需要的水超过可获得或可安全使用的水量——甚至彻底短缺。

structure.text: according to data released by the United Nations
the freshwater resources of more than half the countries across the globe
when people increasingly demand more water than is available or safe for use

difficult_points: released by the United Nations 是过去分词短语，修饰 data。
either stress ... or outright shortages 构成选择结构，表示从水资源压力到彻底短缺。
than is available or safe for use 是比较结构，than 后省略了对应的 water。

sentences.sentence: By mid-century, as much as three-quarters of the earth's population could face scarcities of freshwater.
sentences.translation: 到本世纪中叶，地球上多达四分之三的人口可能面临淡水匮乏。

structure.text: as much as three-quarters of the earth's population

difficult_points: as much as 在数字前强调数量之大，表示“多达”。
could 表示对未来可能情况的预测，而不是已经发生的事实。

sentences.sentence: . Scientists expect water scarcity to become more common in large part because the world's population is rising and many people are getting richer (thus expanding demand) and because global climate change is exacerbating aridity and reducing supply in many regions.
sentences.translation: 科学家预计水资源短缺会越来越普遍，这在很大程度上是因为世界人口不断增长、许多人日益富裕（因而扩大了需求），也因为全球气候变化正在加剧干旱并减少许多地区的供应。

structure.text: expect water scarcity to become more common
because the world's population is rising and many people are getting richer
and because global climate change is exacerbating aridity and reducing supply in many regions

difficult_points: expect water scarcity to become 是“expect + 宾语 + 不定式”结构。
两个 because 结构并列，分别给出人口与财富增长、气候变化两组原因。
thus expanding demand 是现在分词短语，说明人们变富所带来的结果。

sentences.sentence: What is more, many water sources are threatened by faulty waste disposal, releases of industrial pollutants, fertilizer runoff, and coastal influxes of saltwater into aquifers as groundwater is depleted.
sentences.translation: 此外，许多水源还受到不当废物处理、工业污染物排放、化肥径流以及地下水枯竭时沿海咸水涌入含水层等因素的威胁。

structure.text: are threatened by faulty waste disposal, releases of industrial pollutants, fertilizer runoff, and coastal influxes of saltwater into aquifers

difficult_points: What is more 是连接性插入语，表示在前述原因之外再补充一点。
are threatened by 是被动结构，后接四类威胁来源。
as groundwater is depleted 表示随着地下水被抽空，咸水会侵入含水层。

sentences.sentence: . Because lack of access to water can lead to starvation, disease, political instability, and even armed conflict, failure to take action can have broad and grave consequences.
sentences.translation: 由于无法获得水可能导致饥饿、疾病、政治动荡甚至武装冲突，不采取行动会造成广泛而严重的后果。

structure.text: Because lack of access to water can lead to starvation, disease, political instability, and even armed conflict
can have broad and grave consequences

difficult_points: Because 引导原因状语从句，主句主语是 failure to take action。
lack of access to water 指无法得到水，而不只是水量减少。
failure to take action 是名词化表达，意为“未能采取行动”。

sentences.sentence: Fortunately, to a great extent, the technologies and policy tools required to conserve existing freshwater and to secure more of it are known among which several seem particularly effective.
sentences.translation: 幸运的是，在很大程度上，人们已经掌握了保护现有淡水并获得更多淡水所需的技术和政策工具，其中有几种似乎尤为有效。

structure.text: required to conserve existing freshwater and to secure more of it
among which several seem particularly effective

difficult_points: required to ... 是过去分词短语，修饰 technologies and policy tools。
两个不定式 to conserve 与 to secure 并列，说明这些手段的用途。
among which 引导定语从句，which 指前面的 technologies and policy tools。

sentences.sentence: What is needed now is action.
sentences.translation: 现在需要的是行动。
difficult_points: What is needed now 是 what 引导的主语从句，整体充当主语。
is 后的 action 是表语，强调所缺的不是知识而是行动。

sentences.sentence: Governments and authorities at every level have to formulate and execute plans for implementing the political, economic, and technological measures that can ensure water security now and in the coming decades.
sentences.translation: 各级政府和主管部门必须制定并执行计划，落实能够保障当前及未来几十年水安全的政治、经济和技术措施。

structure.text: Governments and authorities at every level
have to formulate and execute plans
for implementing the political, economic, and technological measures
that can ensure water security now and in the coming decades

difficult_points: formulate and execute 是两个并列谓语，分别表示制定和执行。
for implementing ... 修饰 plans，说明计划的实施目标。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 measures。

sentences.sentence: . The world's water problems require, as a start, an understanding of how much freshwater each person requires, along with knowledge of the factors that impede supply and increase demand in different parts of the world.
sentences.translation: 要解决世界水资源问题，首先必须了解每个人需要多少淡水，同时还要知道世界不同地区有哪些因素会阻碍供应并增加需求。

structure.text: an understanding of how much freshwater each person requires
along with knowledge of the factors that impede supply and increase demand in different parts of the world
that impede supply and increase demand in different parts of the world

difficult_points: how much freshwater each person requires 是嵌在介词 of 后的宾语从句。
along with 连接 understanding 与 knowledge 两项必要认识。
that 引导定语从句修饰 factors，其中 impede 与 increase 是并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: Main Falkenmark of the Stockholm International Water Institute and other experts estimate that, on average, each person on the earth needs a minimum of 1000 cubic meters (m3) of water.
sentences.translation: 斯德哥尔摩国际水研究所的 Main Falkenmark 和其他专家估计，平均而言，地球上每个人至少需要1000立方米的水。

structure.text: Main Falkenmark of the Stockholm International Water Institute and other experts
that, on average, each person on the earth needs a minimum of 1000 cubic meters (m3) of water

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，给出专家估算的具体内容。
on average 是插入状语，表示数字是平均值。
a minimum of 强调这是每人的最低需求量。

sentences.sentence: The minimum water each person requires for drinking, hygiene, and growing food.
sentences.translation: 这是每个人用于饮用、卫生和种植食物所需的最低水量。

structure.text: for drinking, hygiene, and growing food

difficult_points: 原文是一个名词性残句，语义承接上一句的最低用水量。
each person requires 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 water。
for 后并列 drinking、hygiene 和 growing food 三种用途。

sentences.sentence: The volume is equivalent to two-fifths of an Olympic-size swimming pool.
sentences.translation: 这一水量相当于一个奥运会标准泳池容量的五分之二。

structure.text: two-fifths of an Olympic-size swimming pool

difficult_points: The volume 回指上文的1000立方米最低用水量。
two-fifths of 表示整体容量的五分之二。

sentences.sentence: . Much of the Americas and northern Eurasia enjoy abundant water supplies.
sentences.translation: 美洲大部分地区和欧亚大陆北部拥有丰富的水资源。

structure.text: Much of the Americas and northern Eurasia

difficult_points: Much of 修饰由 the Americas and northern Eurasia 构成的地区范围。
enjoy 在这里不是“享受”的主观感受，而是表示“拥有有利条件”。

sentences.sentence: But several regions are beset by greater or lesser degrees of "physical" scarcity-whereby demand exceeds local availability.
sentences.translation: 但有些地区受到程度不一的“物理性”缺水困扰，也就是需求超过当地可供水量。

structure.text: are beset by greater or lesser degrees of "physical" scarcity

difficult_points: are beset by 是被动结构，表示某些地区持续受缺水问题困扰。
greater or lesser degrees of 表示严重程度有高有低。
whereby 引出对 physical scarcity 的解释，即需求超过本地可供量。

sentences.sentence: Other areas, among them Central Africa, parts of the Indian subcontinent, and Southeast Asia contend with "economic" water scarcity limit access even though sufficient supplies are available.
sentences.translation: 另一些地区，包括中非、印度次大陆部分地区和东南亚，则要应对“经济性”缺水：即使水源充足，获取水资源的能力仍受到限制。

structure.text: among them Central Africa, parts of the Indian subcontinent, and Southeast Asia
even though sufficient supplies are available

difficult_points: among them ... 是插入性列举，具体说明 Other areas。
contend with 表示不得不面对并处理一种困难。
even though 引导让步状语从句；原文在 scarcity 与 limit 之间存在转录缺漏，但需保持原样。

sentences.sentence: More than half of the precipitation that falls on land is never available for capture or storage because it evaporates from the ground or transpires from plants; this fraction is called blue-water sources-rivers, lakes, wetlands, and aquifers-that people can tap directly.
sentences.translation: 超过一半落在陆地上的降水从来无法被收集或储存，因为它会从地面蒸发或经植物蒸腾；这一部分被称为蓝水来源——河流、湖泊、湿地和含水层——人们可以直接取用。

structure.text: More than half of the precipitation
is never available for capture or storage
because it evaporates from the ground or transpires from plants
this fraction is called blue-water sources-rivers, lakes, wetlands, and aquifers

difficult_points: that falls on land 是定语从句，限定 precipitation。
because 引导原因状语从句，其中 evaporates 与 transpires 描述水分离开地表的两种方式。
分号连接两个独立分句；末尾 that 引导定语从句，说明这些水源可被直接利用。

expressions.text: be available for capture or storage

sentences.sentence: Farm irrigation from these free-flowing bodies is the biggest single human use of freshwater resources, but the intense local demand they create often drains the surroundings of ready supplies.
sentences.translation: 利用这些自由流动水体进行农田灌溉，是人类对淡水资源最大的一项用途，但它所造成的强烈本地需求常常会耗尽周边现成的水源。

structure.text: Farm irrigation from these free-flowing bodies
is the biggest single human use of freshwater resources
often drains the surroundings of ready supplies

difficult_points: but 连接两个转折分句，前者说明灌溉用途最大，后者指出其代价。
they create 是省略关系代词 that 的定语从句，修饰 demand，they 回指灌溉用水活动或水体。
drain A of B 表示把 A 中的 B 耗尽。

sentences.sentence: . Lots of water, but not always where it is needed one hundred and ten thousand cubic kilometers of precipitation, nearly 10 times the volume of Lake Superior, falls from the sky onto the earth's land surface every year.
sentences.translation: 水量很多，却并不总出现在需要它的地方：每年有十一万立方千米的降水从天空落到地球陆地表面，接近苏必利尔湖水量的10倍。

structure.text: Lots of water, but not always where it is needed
one hundred and ten thousand cubic kilometers of precipitation
nearly 10 times the volume of Lake Superior
falls from the sky onto the earth's land surface every year

difficult_points: 句首 Lots of water ... 是图表式标题，原文与后面的完整句子连写。
nearly 10 times the volume of Lake Superior 是同位补充，说明降水总量的规模。
where it is needed 是 where 引导的地点从句，it 指 water。

expressions.text: not always where it is needed
expressions.meaning_zh: 并不总在需要它的地方

sentences.sentence: This huge quantity would easily fulfill the requirements of everyone on the planet if the water arrived where and when people needed it.
sentences.translation: 如果这些水能在人们需要的地点和时间到来，如此巨大的水量足以轻松满足地球上所有人的需求。

structure.text: would easily fulfill the requirements of everyone on the planet
if the water arrived where and when people needed it
where and when people needed it

difficult_points: would 与 if 从句中的过去式 arrived 构成对现实条件的假设。
where and when 并列，分别表示所需的地点和时间。
it 在从句末尾回指 the water。

sentences.sentence: But much of it cannot be captured (top), and the rest is disturbed unevenly (bottom).
sentences.translation: 但其中很大一部分无法收集（上图），其余部分的分布也不均匀（下图）。

structure.text: and the rest is disturbed unevenly

difficult_points: it 回指上一句的巨大降水量，the rest 指可收集部分之外的剩余水量。
cannot be captured 与 is disturbed 均为被动结构；原文 disturbed 很可能是 distributed 的转录错误，需保持原样。
top 和 bottom 是对配图上下部分的指示。

sentences.sentence: Green water (61.1% of total precipitation): absorbed by soil and plants, then released back into the air: unavailable for withdrawal.
sentences.translation: 绿水（占总降水量的61.1%）：被土壤和植物吸收，随后释放回空气中，因此无法抽取利用。

structure.text: then released back into the air

difficult_points: 这是图表说明式省略句，absorbed 和 released 前省略了 is。
then released ... 与 absorbed ... 并列，说明绿水先被吸收、后回到空气的过程。
unavailable for withdrawal 表示无法通过抽取供人直接使用。

sentences.sentence: Blue water (38.8% of total precipitation): collected in rivers, lakes, wetlands, and groundwater: available for withdrawal before it evaporates or reaches the ocean.
sentences.translation: 蓝水（占总降水量的38.8%）：汇集在河流、湖泊、湿地和地下水中，在蒸发或流入海洋前可以抽取利用。

structure.text: collected in rivers, lakes, wetlands, and groundwater
before it evaporates or reaches the ocean

difficult_points: 这是与上一句平行的图表说明式省略句，collected 前省略了 is。
before 引导时间状语从句，限定可抽取利用的时间窗口。
it 指 Blue water，evaporates 与 reaches 是并列谓语。

sentences.sentence: These figures may not add up to 100% because of rounding.
sentences.translation: 由于四舍五入，这些数字相加可能不等于100%。
difficult_points: These figures 回指绿水61.1%和蓝水38.8%这两个比例。
because of 后接名词 rounding，表示数值未正好合计为100%的原因。

sentences.sentence: Only 1.5% is directly used by people.
sentences.translation: 只有1.5%的水被人们直接使用。
difficult_points: Only 放在比例前强调人类直接使用的份额非常小。
is directly used 是被动结构，by people 点明使用者。

sentences.sentence: . Waters run away in tremendous wildfires in recent years.
sentences.translation: 近年来，巨大的野火使水资源流失。
difficult_points: run away 在这里描述水资源因野火而流失，并非人主动逃跑。
in recent years 给出时间范围；原句措辞带有转录痕迹，需按原文理解。

sentences.sentence: The economic actors had all taken their share reasonably enough: they just did not consider the needs of the natural environment, which suffered greatly when its inadequate supply was reduced to critical levels by drought.
sentences.translation: 各经济参与者拿走各自份额本身看似相当合理；他们只是没有考虑自然环境的需求，而自然环境原本不足的供水又因干旱降至危急水平，因而遭受重创。

structure.text: had all taken their share reasonably enough
they just did not consider the needs of the natural environment
when its inadequate supply was reduced to critical levels by drought

difficult_points: 冒号后的分句解释前述分配看似合理却存在的问题。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，指代 the natural environment。
when 引导时间状语从句，its 指自然环境，was reduced 是被动结构。

sentences.sentence: The members of the Murray-Darling Basin Commission are now frantically trying to extricate themselves from the disastrous results of their misallocation of the total water resource.
sentences.translation: 墨累—达令盆地委员会的成员如今正竭力设法摆脱因错误分配全部水资源而造成的灾难性后果。

structure.text: The members of the Murray-Darling Basin Commission
to extricate themselves from the disastrous results
of their misallocation of the total water resource

difficult_points: are ... trying 使用现在进行时，强调当前正在紧急补救。
to extricate themselves 作 trying 的不定式宾语，表示使自己摆脱困境。
of their misallocation ... 说明灾难性后果的来源。

sentences.sentence: Given the difficulties of sensibly apportioning the water supply within a single nation, imagine the complexities of doing so for international river basins such as that of the Jordan River, which borders on Lebanon, Syria, Israel, the Palestinian areas, and Jordan, all of which have claims to the shared, but limited, supply in an extremely parched region.
sentences.translation: 既然在一个国家内部合理分配水源都如此困难，不妨想象一下在约旦河流域这类国际河流流域中这样做有多复杂：该流域毗邻黎巴嫩、叙利亚、以色列、巴勒斯坦地区和约旦，而这些地方都对极度干旱地区中有限的共享水源提出权利主张。

structure.text: Given the difficulties of sensibly apportioning the water supply within a single nation
of doing so for international river basins such as that of the Jordan River
which borders on Lebanon, Syria, Israel, the Palestinian areas, and Jordan
all of which have claims to the shared, but limited, supply in an extremely parched region

difficult_points: Given ... 是句首条件背景，意为“考虑到……”。
doing so 中 so 代替 sensibly apportioning the water supply。
that of the Jordan River 中 that 代替 river basin；两个 which 从句分别说明流域毗邻地区及这些地区的水权主张。

sentences.sentence: The struggle for freshwater has contributed to civil and military disputes in the area.
sentences.translation: 对淡水的争夺已经加剧了该地区的民事和军事争端。

structure.text: civil and military disputes in the area

difficult_points: 现在完成时 has contributed 表示这种影响从过去延续到现在。
contribute to 在此表示成为争端的促成因素，而非唯一原因。

sentences.sentence: Only continuing negotiations and compromises have kept this tense situation under control.
sentences.translation: 只有持续的谈判与妥协才使这一紧张局势维持在可控范围内。
difficult_points: Only 修饰主语，强调谈判与妥协是控制局势的唯一手段，但不引发倒装。
keep A under control 是“keep + 宾语 + 补语”结构，表示使局势保持可控。
